mission.title: serverless-workflow: Authentication are not referenceable
mission.description: Authentication are not referenceable. Requested by 2+ users.

steps.title: Check current serverless-workflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your serverless-workflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
helm list -n serverless-workflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working serverless-workflow installation.

steps.title: Review serverless-workflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant serverless-workflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
kubectl get configmap -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=serverless-workflow
```
**What seems off**:

Authentication are not referenceable

**What you expected to be**:

Authentications defined at top level should be referenceable

After discussing the issue with , and well aware than the reference system previously in place was

steps.title: Apply the fix for Authentication are not referenceable
steps.description:
- Fixes #904 
- Partially addresses #866
- Changes  the way authentication policies can be referenced
- Changes the way authentication policies can use secret
- Adds a new `secrets` runtime expression argument
```yaml
use:
  authentications:
    basicTest:
      basic:
        username: test
        password: test
do:
  - getPetById:
      call: openapi
      with:
        operationId: getPetById
        parameters:
          id: 69
        authentication: 
          use: basicTest
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n serverless-workflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=serverless-workflow
kubectl get events -n serverless-workflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Authentication are not referenceable" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- Fixes #904 
- Partially addresses #866
- Changes  the way authentication policies can be referenced
- Changes the way authentication policies can use secret
- Adds a new `secrets` runtime expression argument
codeSnippets:
use:
  authentications:
    basicTest:
      basic:
        username: test
        password: test
do:
  - getPetById:
      call: openapi
      with:
        operationId: getPetById
        parameters:
          id: 69
        authentication: 
          use: basicTest

document: {}
use:
  secrets:
    - foo
  configure:
    bar: 1
do:
  - task1:
       set:
         size: ${ $configuration.size }

document: {}
use:
  secrets:
    - foo
  constants:
    bar: 1
do:
  - task1:
       set:
         size: ${ $constants.size }
         shape: ${ $secrets.foo }

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with serverless-workflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
